## Summary

Content Conquered's 13-point pre-publish checklist is the closest existing artifact to the canonical 12-point checklist the command brief specifies. While the stinger will encode its own 12-point version, this checklist provides the best-researched existing framework to draw from. The article version (not just the PDF) provides detailed explanations for each standard, making it a valuable grounding document for the stinger's own checklist design.

The 13 standards cover: (1) spelling/grammar/factual errors; (2) voice, styling, and formatting consistency; (3) data points backed by reputable, timely sources; (4) delivering on title/heading promises; (5) avoiding word bloat; (6) brand tone/positioning alignment; (7) audience experience level accuracy; (8) thought leadership positioning; (9) unique perspective/take; (10) readability and enjoyment; (11) no unanswered questions; (12) no plagiarism; (13) special instructions (images, internal links, SEO keywords, CTAs, stakeholder sign-off).

Key insight for the stinger: Standard #3 (data point verification) emphasizes not trusting "data point round-up articles" but instead tracing statistics to original sources. Standard #9 (unique perspective) requires asking "does reading this feel worthwhile?" - not just "is this accurate?" Standard #13 is explicitly the CTA/internal links/images gate, confirming that these elements belong in a final checklist pass rather than being treated as optional.

The guide notes a minimum two-person review process for all content (writer + PM or editor), even for small teams. For solo founders, this translates to a time-gap self-review: write, let it rest, review as a reader.

## Key data points / quotes

- 13 quality standards used by Content Conquered's own review process before client delivery.
- Standard #3: "Be especially careful of data points that sound too good to be true or that come from 'data point round-up articles.'" Trace to original source.
- Standard #4: "If you're promising an 'Ultimate Guide,' failing to deliver one could hurt your brand's reputation!"
- Standard #5: If content "feels more fluffy than substantive, work with subject matter experts to increase its depth."
- Standard #9: Unique perspective test - "Does reading this feel worthwhile?"
- Standard #12: Plagiarism check goes beyond Copyscape - check that you're not "accidentally plagiarizing by rewording existing articles."
- Standard #13: Final check - images, internal/external links, SEO keywords, CTAs, stakeholder sign-off.
- Review process: "at least two people review every piece of content before it goes out" (writer + PM or editor).
- Solo founder equivalent: time-gap self-review (write, rest, review as a reader).

## Implications for stinger-forge

- This is the primary source for designing the canonical 12-point checklist in `guides/06-pre-publish-checklist.md` - the stinger's version should map to these 13 standards and consolidate where appropriate.
- Standard #13 (CTAs, internal links, images, keywords) confirms these are final-gate items, not mid-draft concerns - structure the checklist accordingly.
- The "data round-up articles" warning should be in the fact-checking section of the checklist.
- The two-person review principle should be translated to a "time-gap self-review" protocol for solo founders in the checklist guide.
- Standard #5 (fluff over substance) maps to the word count heuristic: post length should be driven by informational completeness, not keyword targets or arbitrary minimums.
